commands.command: re-review — real behavior proof ci now passes (completed/success). proof section present with all 6 required fields
commands.freeform_prompt: — Real behavior proof CI now passes (completed/success). Proof section present with all 6 required fields.

target.title: fix(gateway): replace blocking sync I/O with async in auth/credential paths (fixes #78805)
target.body:
## Problem
Gateway auth and credential resolution paths perform synchronous I/O (keychain reads, credential-file reads, config-command resolution) that block the Node.js event loop, causing Gateway/Telegram stalls under load.

Fixes #78805

## Root Cause
`readKeychainValue()` and `readCredentialFile()` used synchronous `fs.readFileSync()` and blocking keychain calls instead of async equivalents. `resolveConfigValue()` also blocked during credential resolution.

## Fix
Add async credential and config-resolution helpers (`readKeychainValueAsync`, `readCredentialFileAsync`, `resolveConfigValueAsync`, etc.) and switch CLI auth epoch and auth-storage calls to await the async variants.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Synchronous I/O in auth/credential paths blocked the Node.js event loop, causing latency spikes under concurrent load. After the fix, async I/O yields the event loop and gateway remains responsive.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux (Node.js 22), openclaw development checkout with pnpm, vitest 4.1.8.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.test.ts src/agents/sessions/auth-storage.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
```
$ node ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.test.ts src/agents/sessions/auth-storage.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/user/openclaw

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
   Duration  5.51s
```
- **Observed result after fix**: All 37 credential and auth storage tests pass, confirming no regressions from the async migration. The async helpers (`readKeychainValueAsync`, `readCredentialFileAsync`, `resolveConfigValueAsync`) yield the event loop correctly while preserving existing behavior for credential resolution, auth epoch checks, and session auth storage.
- **What was not tested**: Live gateway under concurrent Telegram load with real keychain and credential files was not manually tested. The tests verify the async migration preserves correctness in the auth/credential resolution code paths.
target.author: mazhuima
